title: Mastra Agent Registration — Every Internal Agent = Mastra Agent

tasks.description: Create MastraRegistryService that owns the Mastra singleton and exposes register/unregister/get methods for Mastra Agent instances.
steps: Create `backend/src/mastra/mastra-registry.service.ts` as an @Injectable NestJS service
Initialize a `Mastra` instance (from `@mastra/core`) in the constructor with empty agents: `new Mastra({ agents: {} })`

Expose `registerAgent(row: AgentRow)`: construct `new Agent({ id: row.id, name: row.name, instructions: row.instructions, model: row.llm_model || 'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6' })`, then call `this.mastra.addAgent(agent, row.id)`. Note: `addAgent()` silently skips if key exists — this is fine for startup but NOT for updates.
Expose `unregisterAgent(id: string): boolean`: calls `this.mastra.removeAgent(id)`, returns the boolean result
Expose `getAgent(id: string): Agent | null`: wrap `this.mastra.getAgent(id)` in try/catch — `getAgent()` THROWS MastraError if not found, so catch and return `null`
Expose `listRegistered(): { count: number; agentIds: string[] }`: calls `Object.keys(this.mastra.listAgents())` for the keys, returns count + ids

Define a local `AgentRow` interface matching the raw pg query row shape: `{ id: string; name: string; instructions: string; llm_model: string | null; source: string; ... }` — only the fields needed for Agent construction

tasks.description: Create MastraModule that provides and exports MastraRegistryService globally, and wire it into AppModule.
steps: Create `backend/src/mastra/mastra.module.ts` as a `@Global()` `@Module`
Register `MastraRegistryService` as a provider and export it
Import `ConfigModule` so the service can access `DATABASE_URL` via `ConfigService`
In `backend/src/app.module.ts`, add `MastraModule` to the imports array — place it BEFORE `AgentsModule` and `AgentPreviewModule` so the registry is available when those modules initialize

tasks.description: Load all existing mastra-source agents from the DB into the registry on application startup, using OnApplicationBootstrap to avoid race conditions with AgentsService's OnModuleInit pg Pool.
steps: In `MastraRegistryService`, implement `OnApplicationBootstrap` interface
Inject `ConfigService` and create a `Pool` instance from `DATABASE_URL` in `onApplicationBootstrap()`
Query `SELECT id, name, instructions, llm_model, source FROM agents WHERE source = 'mastra'` — no user_id filter, loads seed + user agents
For each row, call `this.registerAgent(row)` inside a try/catch so one failure doesn't block others
Log: `this.logger.log('Mastra registry: loaded ${count}/${total} agents')` using NestJS Logger
Close the Pool after loading — ongoing queries use AgentsService's pool, not this one

tasks.description: Wire AgentsService to sync the Mastra registry on create/update/delete operations.
steps: Inject `MastraRegistryService` into `AgentsService` constructor
In `createAgent()`: after the INSERT RETURNING, if `row.source === 'mastra'`, call `this.registry.registerAgent(row)` — the returned row has all needed fields

In `updateAgent()`: after the UPDATE RETURNING, call `this.registry.unregisterAgent(id)` first (safe even if not found), then if `row.source === 'mastra'` call `this.registry.registerAgent(row)`. This handles source changes (mastra→external removes, external→mastra adds).
In `deleteAgent()`: BEFORE the DELETE query, call `this.registry.unregisterAgent(id)` — ignore the boolean return
No changes needed to `AgentsModule` imports because `MastraModule` is `@Global()`

tasks.description: Refactor AgentPreviewService to use registry agents for saved agents while keeping throwaway instances for unsaved wizard previews.
steps: Inject `MastraRegistryService` into `AgentPreviewService` constructor
Add `@IsOptional() @IsString() agentId?: string` field to `AgentPreviewDto` in `backend/src/agents/preview/agent-preview.dto.ts`

In `streamPreview()`: if `dto.agentId` is provided, call `this.registry.getAgent(dto.agentId)` — if it returns an Agent, use it for `.stream()`. If null (not in registry), fall back to throwaway `new Agent()` with the dto fields.
If `dto.agentId` is NOT provided (wizard preview), keep existing throwaway `new Agent({ id: 'preview', ... })` behavior unchanged
Update `AgentPreviewModule` imports to ensure `MastraRegistryService` is accessible (should already work via @Global)

tasks.description: Add GET /agents/registry/status endpoint for debugging — shows how many agents are loaded in the Mastra registry and their IDs.
steps: In `AgentsController`, add `@Get('registry/status')` handler that calls `this.registry.listRegistered()` and returns the result
Inject `MastraRegistryService` into `AgentsController` constructor alongside `AgentsService`
CRITICAL: Place the `@Get('registry/status')` route ABOVE `@Get(':id')` in the controller — NestJS matches routes top-to-bottom, and `:id` would catch 'registry' as a param otherwise
Return shape: `{ count: number, agentIds: string[] }`

tasks.description: Unit test MastraRegistryService — verify register/unregister/get behavior and the critical getAgent-throws-on-missing edge case.
steps: Create `backend/src/mastra/mastra-registry.service.spec.ts`
Test: `registerAgent()` with a mock row → `getAgent(id)` returns an Agent with matching id, name, instructions
Test: `getAgent()` for non-existent id returns `null` (not throw) — verifies the try/catch wrapper works
Test: `unregisterAgent()` returns `true` for registered, `false` for non-existent

Test: `registerAgent()` twice with same id → second call is silently skipped (addAgent behavior), agent retains original config. Then test the update pattern: `unregisterAgent()` + `registerAgent()` → agent has new config.
Test: `listRegistered()` returns correct count after register/unregister operations
No DB mocking needed — only test the in-memory Mastra registry, skip the onApplicationBootstrap DB loading
